description_long: This is a commercial jet airliner with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily in white, blue, and red, featuring the BEA (British European Airways) livery. The wings are low-mounted and swept back, with red upper surfaces and visible control surfaces. The aircraft has three rear-mounted jet engines, two on either side of the fuselage and one integrated into the tail, and a distinctive T-tail configuration. The landing gear is tricycle-type, with the main gear under the wings and a nose gear visible. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows for pilot visibility. The tail fin displays a stylized Union Jack, and the overall design is typical of mid-20th-century commercial jets.
description: A classic commercial jet airliner in BEA livery, featuring swept low wings, three rear-mounted jet engines, and a T-tail. The fuselage is streamlined with a forward cockpit and tricycle landing gear.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, red wing surfaces, and T-tail configuration.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the BEA logo, cockpit windows, and three rear-mounted engines.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the T-tail, engine arrangement, and the word 'CARGO' on the wing.
description_view_3: Right side profile with clear visibility of the tail fin's Union Jack design and the aircraft's overall proportions.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back configuration with no visible winglets and a slight dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Three rear-mounted jet engines, two on the fuselage sides and one integrated into the tail.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, typical of commercial airliners.
